capability_objective: The support lead is closing a duplicate intake before the overnight queue is handed off. Cancel “Duplicate Invoice Attachment Case” (sup-275) because the requester confirmed it duplicates canonical case sup-611. Use the supplied stable ID; do not search by title or touch “Invoice Attachment Missing Case” (sup-611) or the similarly named record at “Cedar Arts Sandbox”. If the cancellation times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not issue a second cancellation: retrieve sup-275 first. If that get verifies cancelled, stop. Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, or notify. Keep the reply under 55 words and explicitly distinguish the timeout from the later verified outcome.
prompt:
The support lead is closing a duplicate intake before the overnight queue is handed off. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `SUPPORT.md`, then use the genuine local `./supportctl` executable through Pi's Bash tool for support operations. You may run its built-in help before any data operation. Treat the executable as the sole interface to support data. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing database, generated runtime data, the tool-managed receipt, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit any of those files. Do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the support workflow.

Cancel “Duplicate Invoice Attachment Case” (`sup-275`) because the requester confirmed it duplicates canonical case `sup-611`. Use the supplied stable ID directly and do not search by title. Do not touch `sup-611` or the similarly named record at “Cedar Arts Sandbox”. Make exactly one cancellation attempt. If that attempt times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not cancel again: retrieve `sup-275` by stable ID first. If the retrieval verifies that it is cancelled, stop support operations. Do not infer the outcome from the cancellation error alone.

Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, search, create, update, or notify. Do not retrieve any record except `sup-275`, and do not make any support-data call beyond the one cancellation attempt and the one verification retrieval permitted above. Leave every other case, preference, availability entry, notification, audit event, receipt, and protected file untouched. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete operation evidence. Leave no scratch artifacts.

Create `cancellation-result.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, case title, workspace name, raw error text, or other prose. In the first sentence, state that the single cancellation attempt for `sup-275` timed out and left commit state uncertain. In the second, state that a later retrieval verified `sup-275` as cancelled, that no second cancellation was attempted, and that no notification was sent. Keep the complete response under 55 words and return exactly the file's two sentences as the final response. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the executable, documentation, receipt, verifier, and protected environment unchanged.
